docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Violeta Damjanovic-Behrendt

Founder & CTO of GreenTwin GmbH

[YOUR TAGLINE — e.g., Turning spatial data into insights | GIS | Remote Sensing | Python]

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I hold a PhD in Computer Science (2008) from the University of Belgrade (Serbia).

My professional carrier began in the financial sector, where I worked as a software developer and data analyst (1998–2004), followed by a role as software project manager (2004–2006). Alongside my industry experience in financial technologies, I pursued both my MSc and PhD studies. My MSc thesis was awarded the 2005 Research Prize by the Serbian Society for Informatics, making the beginning of my transition towards a research career.

In 2006, I joined Salzburg Research (Austria), where I contributed to several departments over the years, including Knowledge and Media Technologies (2006–2014), the Internet of Things Group (2014–2020), Smart Digital Twins (2020–2021), and the Intelligent Connectivity Group (2021–2023). Throughout this period, I was involved in numerous national and European research and innovation projects, advancing digital technologies in areas such as Web agents, Semantic Web technologies, ontology engineering, digital twins, interoperability, and intelligent networked systems.

In 2022, I founded GreenTwin GmbH with the goal of transforming research results and innovative ideas into practical solutions addressing supply chain resilience, sustainability, and digital transformation challenges. This entrepreneurial journey led me to explore the potential of remote sensing, geospatial intelligence, and Earth Observation data analytics for supply chain optimization, risk assessment, and decision support.

Today, my professional portfolio reflects a unique combination of industrial practice, scientific research, and technology entrepreneurship, with a strong focus on developing data-driven solutions that bridge emerging technologies and real-world societal and industrial needs.

View My Projects Download CV

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
• Semantic Web & Ontology Engineering

    • RDF, OWL
    • SPARQL (SPARQL Protocol and RDF Query Language) - query language and protocol for Semantic Web technologies

  • Programming ---

    • Next.js
    • React, React Email, React Resend, Leaflet
    • TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Shadcn UI
    • SQL, PostgreSQL + PostGIS
    • R — sf, terra, ggplot2
    • Python — GeoPandas, NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib

  • GIS & Remote Sensing

    • QGIS, ArcGIS Pro, Google Earth Engine
    • GDAL / OGR, GRASS GIS
    • Multispectral and SAR image analysis
    • Cloud Native Geospatial (COG, STAC, Zarr)

  • Machine Learning & GeoAI

    • Supervised classification — Random Forest, XGBoost
    • Deep learning for image segmentation — U-Net, SAM
    • scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow
    • Object detection in satellite imagery

  • Web Mapping & Data

    • Leaflet.js, Folium, MapLibre GL JS
    • Cloud storage — AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats — GeoTIFF, GeoParquet, NetCDF
    • Streamlit for data-driven web apps

  • Data & Cloud

    • PostgreSQL + PostGIS
    • Cloud storage: AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats: GeoJSON, GeoTIFF, NetCDF, Zarr, GeoParquet

Download CV

docs.title: CTO & Principal Scientist — GreenTwin GmbH
docs.text:
January 2022 – Present | Austria

  • Leading technical strategy and scientific research at GreenTwin GmbH, focused on digital twins, sustainability, and supply chain resilience.
  • Contributing to the EU Horizon Europe project ResC4EU (Resilient Supply Chains for Europe, 2024–2026).
  • Applying Earth Observation data, LLM/SLM architectures, and geospatial stacks to enterprise sustainability challenges.

docs.title: Independent Expert — European Commission (Horizon Europe & H2020)
docs.text:
January 2016 – Present | Remote

  • Serving as independent expert evaluator for the Horizon Europe Programme (including MSCA) since January 2021.
  • Previously evaluated projects under the H2020 Research and Innovation Programme (January 2016 – December 2020).

docs.title: Senior Researcher — Salzburg Research Forschungsgesellschaft
docs.text:
November 2006 – February 2024 | Salzburg, Austria

  • Conducted research across three groups over 17+ years: Knowledge and Media Technologies (KMT), Internet of Things (IoT), and Intelligent Connectivity.
  • Led and contributed to multiple EU-funded projects including EFPF, NIMBLE, IoT4CPS, and EAGLE, focusing on IoT platforms, digital twins, semantic interoperability, and federated systems.
  • Published extensively in international journals and conferences; received Best Paper Award (CogSIMA 2020) and Most Innovative Solution Award (I-ESA 2018).

docs.title: External Lecturer — Salzburg University of Applied Sciences
docs.text:
October 2011 – July 2013 | Salzburg, Austria

  • Taught a BSc course on Game Theory for the Smart Grid (2013).
  • Taught a Master's course on Artificial Intelligence and Algorithmic Game Theory in the MultiMedia Technology (MMT) Department (2011).

docs.title: Software Project Manager (E-Banking) — Postal Savings Bank
docs.text:
December 1998 – December 2006 | Belgrade, Serbia

  • Managed software projects for e-banking services (2004–2006).
  • Worked as Programmer Analyst and Application Programmer using PL/I, DB2, CICS, Visual Basic, Java, and OS/390 (1998–2004).

docs.title: PhD in Computer Science
docs.text:
University of Belgrade, Faculty of Organizational Sciences (FON) | 2008

Thesis: "Ambient Intelligence in Adaptive Online Experiments" — covering Ambient Intelligence, Personalized Adaptation, Multi-Agent Systems (MAS), and Technology Enhanced Learning (TEL). Supervised by Prof. Dr. Vladan Devedzic.

docs.title: MSc in Electronic Business
docs.text:
University of Belgrade, Faculty of Organizational Sciences (FON) | 2005

Thesis: "Web Pedagogical Agents: A Touch of Web Intelligence" — awarded the 2005 Research Award by the Serbian Society for Informatics.

docs.title: Specialist Degree in Internet Technologies
docs.text:
University of Belgrade, Faculty of Organizational Sciences (FON) | 2003

Thesis: "Semantic Web, Ontologies and Agents" — focused on Semantic Web and Web Multi-Agent Systems.

docs.title: BSc (Dipl. Ing.) in Information Systems
docs.text:
University of Belgrade, Faculty of Organizational Sciences (FON) | 1998

Thesis: "Using Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) to Evaluate Investment Projects" — focused on Operations Research and Linear Programming.

  • Independent Expert Evaluator — European Commission / H2020 Programme, 2016–2020

docs.title: Sample Notebook
docs.text:
In [1]: Copied! 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import folium
import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import folium In [ ]: Copied! 
# Placeholder time-series data
# Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv")
data = {
    "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
              "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"],
    "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118],
    "metric_b": [ 80,  88,  95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100,  92,  85,  78],
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df.head()
# Placeholder time-series data # Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv") data = { "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"], "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118], "metric_b": [ 80, 88, 95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100, 92, 85, 78], } df = pd.DataFrame(data) df.head() In [ ]: Copied! 
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe()
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe() In [4]: Copied! 
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))

ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue")
ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral")

ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4)) ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue") ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral") ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold") ax.set_xlabel("Month") ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]") ax.legend() ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [7]: Copied! 
# Placeholder point locations
# Replace with your own lat/lon data, e.g. from a GeoDataFrame or CSV
sites = pd.DataFrame({
    "name":  ["Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E"],
    "lat":   [28.61,    28.65,    28.58,    28.70,    28.55],
    "lon":   [77.20,    77.23,    77.17,    77.28,    77.15],
    "value": [142,      168,      95,       210,       87],
})

# Centre the map on the mean location
m = folium.Map(
    location=[sites["lat"].mean(), sites["lon"].mean()],
    zoom_start=11,
    tiles="CartoDB positron",
)

# Add a CircleMarker for each site, scaled by value
for _, row in sites.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row["lat"], row["lon"]],
        radius=row["value"] / 20,          # scale radius to value
        color="steelblue",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"{row['name']}: {row['value']}",
        popup=folium.Popup(
            f"<b>{row['name']}</b><br>Value: {row['value']}",
            max_width=200,
        ),
    ).add_to(m)

m
